title: Require significance before claiming a win
description: A score difference is reported with variance and a significance check, not as a point estimate that may be noise.
body: Use when comparing two systems or versions. Do not declare a change better from a single-run point difference, and avoid ignoring run-to-run variance from sampling temperature or data order. Actions: run multiple seeds or bootstrap the dataset, report a confidence interval, and apply a significance test sized to the dataset. Validation signals include a reported interval and a test that a within-noise difference is not called a win. Risk boundary: shipping on a non-significant difference wastes the eval and may ship a regression; require a meaningful, measured difference before claiming improvement.
